title: Discretization error and order of accuracy
description: Replacing continuous operators with discrete approximations introduces error that shrinks with step size at the method's order.
body: Use when reasoning about the gap between a simulation and the true continuous solution. Do not attribute all error to floating point, since discretization error usually dominates at practical step sizes. Actions: identify the discretization order, predict how error scales with step size, and confirm the scaling in a convergence study. Validation signals include error shrinking at the predicted rate and the dominant error source identified as discretization rather than rounding. Risk boundary: a scheme whose order is lower than intended wastes refinement, so flag an observed order below theory as an implementation defect.
